items.title: World Cup issues still cannot take shine off greatest show on earth
items.summary: And we are off. Three red cards including a VAR intervention. Two goals for co-hosts Mexico in the Estadio Azteca. One nightmare start for South Africa midfielder Yaya Sithole. And an emotional first World Cup goal for Raul Jimenez at the age of 35.

items.title: 'There's a World Cup happening?' Are Americans excited about the tournament?
items.summary: With the World Cup under way, how excited are people across the United States - and do they even know it's on?

items.title: South Korea get off to winning start after rallying from goal down to beat Czechia
items.summary: South Korea rallied from a goal down to defeat the Czech Republic 2-1 and make a winning start to their World Cup Group A campaign, much to the delight of their fans and the sizeable local Mexican support they enjoyed in a festive Guadalajara. items.title: The Joy of Six: forgotten World Cup goals
items.summary: From Ogris’s Florentine charge to Quagliarella’s Ellis Park chip, half a dozen World Cup classics that deserve to be better remembered The first six or seven World Cups certainly are not without their charms, but they are noticeably light on the kind of viscerally spectacular goals that we take for

items.title: ‘Wonder why that is…’ – Fans fume at amount of empty seats in just second World Cup clash of tournament
items.summary: WORLD CUP fans were seething after the second showdown of the tournament was played in front of huge amounts of empty seats. South Korea came from behind to seal a 2-1 win over Czech Republic in Guadalajara. But, after a probe by The Sun found some World Cup venues have sold less than half of...
